funders.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
funders.mission_summary: Gates Foundation's Global Education Program committed USD 240M in 2025 to foundational learning. In December 2025, they co-announced a USD 40M, 4-year AI-education partnership with ADQ specifically targeting sub-Saharan Africa. They are actively searching for programmes combining AI tools with rigorous RCT evidence.
recent_news: Dec 2025: $40M AI-education partnership with ADQ announced for sub-Saharan Africa
2025: $240M Global Education Program commitment
funders.notes: Invite-only. Requires thought leadership visibility as prerequisite. Michael's QJE evidence and SOMA cost architecture positions LearnImpact well. Ideal entry: through the Gates/ADQ AI-education initiative. Long game — 2027-2028 realistic.

funders.name: ADQ (Abu Dhabi Developmental Holding)
funders.mission_summary: UAE sovereign wealth fund and development holding company. Co-announced a USD 40M, 4-year AI-education partnership with the Gates Foundation in December 2025 specifically for AI-enabled foundational learning in sub-Saharan Africa. Actively deploying capital into education technology and AI programmes with rigorous evidence.
recent_news: Dec 2025: $40M AI-education partnership with Gates Foundation announced
funders.notes: PRIMARY TARGET for SOMA. The Gates/ADQ initiative is exactly SOMA's lane. Entry likely requires Gates introduction or thought leadership visibility. Michael needs to be visible in AI-education discourse before approaching.

funders.name: Founders Pledge
funders.mission_summary: UK-based initiative where entrepreneurs pledge a percentage of proceeds to highly effective charities. Funds rigorous, cost-effective interventions with strong evidence. Their education portfolio is oriented toward LAYS-based cost-effectiveness analysis. Currently in active due diligence on LearnImpact.
recent_news: June 2026: Due diligence questions submitted by Lorcan Clarke, deadline June 26
Active ask: USD 5M for SOMA over 4 years
funders.notes: ACTIVE SUBMISSION. Lorcan Clarke is the programme officer. Youdi Schipper handling KiuFunza research questions (2.1-3.2). Michael handling structure, positioning, SOMA questions (1.1-1.3, 4.1-4.2). Decision Q1-Q2 2027. Do not rush. Process is 6-12 months. Top-end budget ask: $5M.

funders.name: Hempel Foundation
funders.mission_summary: LearnImpact's most important long-term anchor donor. Has funded KiuFunza historically. At the May 2026 Steering Committee spontaneously signalled renewal interest. Hearing Committee is the live decision moment for renewal. Contact: Rosa.
recent_news: May 2026: Spontaneous renewal signal at Steering Committee. Hearing Committee is live decision.
funders.notes: ANCHOR DONOR. Do not take for granted. Hearing Committee is the live decision. Rosa manages the relationship — ensure she has everything she needs. Must transition SOMA conversation carefully — do not signal KiuFunza is being phased out before renewal confirmed.

funders.name: Vodacom Tanzania Foundation (VTF)
funders.mission_summary: Corporate foundation of Vodacom Tanzania with an 80% technology mandate. Near-confirmed KiuFunza Phase 5 funder. Has contributed TZS 462M to KiuFunza 2026-27. The technology mandate makes VTF a natural fit for SOMA's AI chatbot component. Must extend conversation to SOMA before KiuFunza phases down.
recent_news: 2026: TZS 462M committed to KiuFunza. Grant Agreement active.
funders.notes: ACTIVE PARTNER. Contact: Sandra Oswald. VTF 80% tech mandate = strong SOMA fit (WhatsApp AI chatbot). SOMA conversation with VTF NOT YET STARTED — this is urgent. Must happen before KiuFunza phases down. Clause 2.3 in current Grant Agreement may need updating re: TIF reallocation.

funders.name: Dovetail / Prevail Fund
funders.mission_summary: USD 100K confirmed for LearnImpact Track 1 (KiuFunza). Contact Ali Ellegard. Assessment technology is the entry point for SOMA conversations. Track 2 (SOMA) is a separate conversation not yet started. Potential 10-year partnership.
recent_news: 2026: USD 100K Track 1 confirmed. Track 2 (SOMA) conversation not started.
funders.notes: ACTIVE PARTNER. Track 2 SOMA conversation must start soon — assessment technology angle. Ali Ellegard is the contact. Potential for long-term (10-year) partnership according to Drive docs.

funders.name: Open Philanthropy
funders.mission_summary: Large US philanthropic organisation with effective altruism principles. Prioritises cost-effectiveness and rigorous evidence (GiveWell-aligned). Global health and wellbeing is a major focus. Education programmes must demonstrate compelling cost-per-outcome metrics in LAYS terms.
known_restrictions: USD 12/LAYS claim must be verified or clearly modelled
recent_news: Ongoing: EA-aligned funders actively seeking education programmes with LAYS evidence
funders.notes: Strong alignment on evidence but SOMA has no published results yet. Position as: 'funding the evidence generation' — the RCT is the product. Mtwara endline (2027) is the unlock. Submit LOI in 2027 when preliminary results available. KiuFunza QJE evidence is the credibility anchor.

funders.name: Arnold Ventures
funders.mission_summary: Houston-based philanthropy with strong commitment to evidence-based policy and RCT-rigorous interventions. Criminal justice, education, and health focus. Education portfolio prioritises interventions with credible causal evidence and potential for government adoption.
geography: US domestic priority but international considered
funders.notes: Good fit — QJE published evidence is the right credential. Position as 'RCT-backed teacher incentive programme seeking to test AI-enabled delivery model'. Government co-financing angle (Kibaha DC) is compelling for policy integration story.

funders.name: Schmidt Futures
funders.mission_summary: Eric Schmidt's philanthropic initiative, focused on AI, science, and education at the intersection of technology and human potential. Strong interest in AI applications that improve educational outcomes, particularly in underserved communities. Prefer novel approaches combining technology with evidence.
recent_news: 2025-2026: Increasing focus on AI applications in education globally
funders.notes: SOMA's WhatsApp AI coaching chatbot is exactly the kind of novel AI application Schmidt funds. Position: 'First AI-coaching intervention delivered via WhatsApp to teachers in rural Tanzania, backed by RCT'. Mtwara will generate publishable evidence. Target 2027 approach after Mtwara baseline data available.

funders.name: William & Flora Hewlett Foundation
funders.mission_summary: One of the world's largest private foundations. Education portfolio focuses on deeper learning, quality education in developing countries, and open educational resources. Strong interest in Sub-Saharan Africa foundational learning. Previously funded TaRL work and evidence-based approaches.
recent_news: 2025: Continued focus on foundational learning in Sub-Saharan Africa
funders.notes: TaRL Africa connection is useful — Hewlett funds TaRL. SOMA builds on TaRL methodology. Useful angle: 'The TaRL methodology has been proven; SOMA is testing whether AI + accountability infrastructure can deliver it at $3.12/student without expensive NGO presence'.

funders.name: Mastercard Foundation
funders.mission_summary: One of the largest foundations in the world. Africa focus. Partners for Learning initiative specifically targets foundational learning in Africa. Wants programmes that work with governments and can reach national scale. Strong interest in evidence and systems change at government level.
recent_news: 2025-2026: Partners for Learning initiative actively seeking foundational learning partners in East Africa
funders.notes: Long-shot but high-value. LearnImpact is small for Mastercard but the Kibaha government co-financing model is exactly what they want to see. Approach in 2027 after Mtwara results and when government integration story is stronger.

funders.name: Global Partnership for Education (GPE)
funders.mission_summary: Multilateral funding mechanism co-hosted by World Bank. Tanzania received an USD 88M GPE grant beginning 2026. GPE is part of the Global Coalition for Foundational Learning and commits USD 15B through 2030. Strong alignment with government education systems and TaRL-adjacent approaches.
known_restrictions: Must align with National Education Sector Plan
recent_news: 2026: Tanzania received $88M GPE grant. Active country programme.
funders.notes: Tanzania has active GPE funding. Entry angle: position SOMA as a complementary NGO delivery mechanism to the GPE-funded government programme. Government co-financing (Kibaha DC) is exactly what GPE wants to see. Requires PMO-RALG relationship as door-opener.

funders.name: FCDO (UK Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office)
funders.mission_summary: UK bilateral development agency. Strong evidence-based mandate. Tanzania is an active FCDO country. Education and girls' learning are strategic priorities. Funds programmes with government partnerships and credible evidence. Competitive procurement process.
recent_news: Post-2025 ODA cuts: FCDO education budget reduced but Tanzania remains active country
funders.notes: Watch for open tenders in Tanzania education. FCDO requires procurement compliance — budget for that effort. QJE evidence is the right credentialing. Note: ODA cuts post-2025 have reduced FCDO Tanzania budget — confirm current priorities before approaching.

funders.name: GIZ (Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit)
funders.mission_summary: German development agency. Active in Tanzania across education, health, and economic development. Funds capacity building, technical assistance, and pilot programmes. Often works through government channels. Prefers implementation partnerships.
funders.notes: GIZ Tanzania has active education programme. Entry via GIZ Dar es Salaam office. Technical assistance model may be a better fit than grant — GIZ could engage LearnImpact as technical service provider for SOMA-adjacent government work.

funders.name: Google.org
funders.mission_summary: Google's philanthropic arm. Provides grants and technical support to nonprofits using technology for social good. Strong interest in AI for social impact, particularly in education and economic opportunity. Rapid response grants and longer-term programme grants available.
recent_news: 2025-2026: Increased focus on AI for social impact grants
funders.notes:
ENTITY DECISION LOCKED (2026-07-01): LearnImpact/SOMA leads Google.org. Timamu deferred — approach separately only after LearnImpact relationship is established.

PREREQUISITES RESOLVED:
- Registration: LearnImpact fully registered (Reg. 00NGO/R/8931)
- Domain: learnimpact.org (main) + learnimpact.or.tz (alias) — secured
- Website: in progress — will carry registration number, mission, board, contact
- NOT a startup: 10+ years KiuFunza evidence, QJE-published +9.4pp, 265 schools, newly independent from Twaweza

TWO-TRACK APPROACH:
Track 1 (immediate): Google for Nonprofits via Goodstack. $10K/month search ad credits + free Workspace. Rolling — apply once website is live. Submit: reg certificate + constitution to Goodstack (10-14 day turnaround).
Track 2 (competitive): Google.org Impact Challenge Africa — cash grant $100K-$2M+. No open call currently. Build relationship with Google.org Africa team BEFORE call opens.

TECH HOOK: Mwalimu WhatsApp AI coaching chatbot. Frame: AI-powered teacher coaching at $3.12/student/year with government co-financing and QJE evidence base. Not a theoretical idea — live Kibaha pilot Term 3 2026.

NEXT STEP: Complete learnimpact.org website → Goodstack submission → Ad Grant → Google.org Africa relationship.

funders.name: Microsoft Philanthropies
funders.mission_summary: Microsoft's philanthropic arm. Focus on AI for humanitarian applications, digital skills, and education technology. Provides grants, Azure cloud credits, and technical partnerships. Interested in AI applications that improve education outcomes at scale.
recent_news: 2025-2026: Microsoft AI for Good programme expanding in Africa
funders.notes: Also explore Azure nonprofit credits for SOMA's cloud infrastructure. Microsoft + SOMA: the Mwalimu chatbot runs on AI infrastructure that Microsoft could host via their nonprofit programme. Grant + in-kind technology partnership is the dual ask.

funders.name: Omidyar Network
funders.mission_summary: Pierre Omidyar's investment and grant-making firm. Luminate spin-off handles civic tech. Omidyar Network funds technology-enabled social change with potential for systemic impact. Education at the nexus of technology and policy is a key focus.
funders.notes: Omidyar funds across the spectrum (grants + equity investments). For SOMA, pure grant approach. Luminate (spun off) focuses on civic tech — not education. Omidyar Network directly is the right target.

funders.name: Skoll Foundation
funders.mission_summary: Jeff Skoll's foundation, focused on social entrepreneurship and scaling proven solutions. Funds Skoll Awardees (proven social entrepreneurs). LearnImpact/Michael could pursue a Skoll Award nomination in 2027-2028 once SOMA has initial evidence.
recent_portfolio: Skoll Awardees across education, health, climate
known_restrictions: Skoll Award — must have proven model, not pilots
Entrepreneurial leader as face of organisation
funders.notes: Skoll Award is a 2028 target, after SOMA has verified evidence. Michael as 'social entrepreneur with QJE-published evidence now scaling to AI-enabled delivery' is the right positioning. Build the track record first.

funders.name: Dubai Cares
funders.mission_summary: UAE-based global philanthropic organisation focused on quality education in developing countries. Partners with organisations in Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Strong interest in foundational learning, school infrastructure, and teacher training.
recent_portfolio: Education programmes across Africa and Asia
funders.notes: UAE connection may be useful given ADQ relationship-building. Relatively accessible compared to Tier 1 funders. Good for KiuFunza continuation proposal while SOMA evidence builds.

funders.name: Aga Khan Foundation (AKF)
funders.mission_summary: Part of the Aga Khan Development Network. Active in East Africa including Tanzania and Kenya. Education Quality Programme focuses on teacher professional development, school improvement, and community engagement. Strong government partnership model.
recent_portfolio: Education Quality Programme in East Africa
funders.notes: AKF Tanzania office in Dar es Salaam. Possible implementation partnership rather than just grant. Teacher professional development is AKF's language — translate SOMA's 'coaching' into their vocabulary.

funders.name: MacArthur Foundation
funders.mission_summary: Chicago-based foundation with 100&Change competition (single $100M grant) and programme grants in education, climate, and nuclear risk reduction. Education portfolio includes foundational learning in Sub-Saharan Africa.
funders.notes: 100&Change is a long shot but extraordinarily valuable. Programme grants more realistic. Monitor for open calls in education. Build relationship through publications and conference presence first.

funders.name: USAID Education
funders.mission_summary: US government bilateral development agency. Education programme in Tanzania historically active. However, 2025-2026 US ODA cuts have dramatically reduced USAID programming globally — Tanzania education funding significantly impacted. Monitor for remaining open opportunities.
known_restrictions: 2025-2026: severe budget cuts make this low priority
funders.detected_priority_shift: 2025: US ODA cut 56.9% — USAID education funding severely reduced globally and in Tanzania
recent_news: 2025: Historic USAID budget cuts under new US administration. Tanzania education programme uncertain.
funders.notes: DEPRIORITISED due to 2025-2026 US ODA cuts. Monitor only. Do not invest significant effort until US development budget stabilizes. If USAID Tanzania education reopens, SOMA is well-positioned.

funders.name: Wellcome Trust
funders.mission_summary: UK health research foundation increasingly interested in the education-health nexus. Funds rigorous research on what improves child development and learning. Education interventions must have strong research design (RCT preferred). Emerging interest in AI tools for health and education.
funders.notes: Out-of-industry angle: frame foundational literacy as health determinant (adult earnings, health literacy, maternal health decisions). Mtwara RCT + partner with UDSM/academic institution. Longer shot but worth monitoring.

funders.name: Luminate (Omidyar Group)
funders.mission_summary: Spun off from Omidyar Network. Focuses on civic empowerment, independent media, and data/digital rights. Emerging interest in AI governance and digital public goods. Less direct fit for education but potential for digital rights + education AI angle.
funders.notes: Weaker fit. Only pursue if SOMA's Mwalimu chatbot is made open source (digital public good angle). Not a priority.

funders.name: World Bank / Education Global Practice
funders.mission_summary: World Bank funds education through government lending and grants. The LAYS metric was developed by the World Bank. Strong interest in programmes that can demonstrate LAYS-based cost-effectiveness. Tanzania is an active World Bank country with existing education operations.
recent_news: 2026: Global Coalition for Foundational Learning — $15B commitment through 2030
funders.notes: World Bank uses LAYS — SOMA's USD 12/LAYS projection is directly their language. Entry: position Michael as thought leader on cost-effective foundational learning in Tanzania. World Bank Tanzania office in Dar es Salaam is entry point.

funders.name: AFD (Agence Française de Développement)
funders.mission_summary: French development agency. Active in East Africa. Education is one of AFD's priority sectors with focus on vocational training and basic education. Less focused on foundational learning specifically but funds education system strengthening.

key_contacts.name: AFD Nairobi or Dar es Salaam

funders.notes: Lower priority. Monitor for call for proposals. Better fit post-SOMA evidence in 2028.

funders.name: UNICEF Tanzania
funders.mission_summary: UN agency for children. Active education programme in Tanzania. Funds learning assessments, teacher training, and government capacity building. Often a convener rather than a funder of NGOs — but implementation partnerships possible.
funders.notes: SOMA uses ASER-adapted assessment — common language with UNICEF. Better as a convener/validator than funder. UNICEF endorsement could be valuable for government conversations even if direct grant is small.

funders.name: Mastercard Foundation
funders.mission_summary: One of the world's largest foundations. EduFinance and Young Africa Works programmes focus on youth economic empowerment across Africa, including digital skills and livelihoods. The creator economy and digital livelihoods angle maps strongly to Timamu's Digital Creation pathway.
funders.language_frame: youth Africa digital-livelihoods systems-change scale economic-inclusion
known_restrictions: Phase 1 organisations unlikely — build track record first
recent_news: 2025-2026: Young Africa Works expanding digital livelihoods focus
funders.notes: TIMAMU FUNDER. Strong thematic fit on creator economy + digital livelihoods. Timamu is Phase 1 — too early for Mastercard's typical grant size. Build track record through 2026-2027, approach in 2028 with evidence of programme delivery. Note: Mastercard Foundation ALSO appears for LearnImpact — no simultaneous approach to same contact.

funders.name: Google.org (Accessibility focus)
funders.mission_summary: Google.org funds organisations using technology to improve lives, including specific programmes for disability inclusion and digital accessibility. Google has committed to making AI tools accessible to persons with disabilities. Strong alignment with Timamu's assistive technology pathway.
recent_news: 2025-2026: Google AI for accessibility commitments expanding
funders.notes: DEFERRED — DO NOT APPROACH (2026-07-01 decision). LearnImpact/SOMA leads with Google.org first. Timamu approaches Google.org separately only AFTER: (a) LearnImpact has an established Google.org relationship, AND (b) Timamu is registered as an independent entity in Tanzania. Timamu registration is currently pending — this is a hard prerequisite for any Google application. Revisit no earlier than 2027.

funders.name: Microsoft Philanthropies (Accessibility)
funders.mission_summary: Microsoft has a specific AI for Accessibility programme committing $25M to NGOs using AI to empower persons with disabilities. Perfect fit for Timamu's assistive technology + creator economy pathways. Also provides Azure credits and technical partnerships.
due_diligence_requirements: Microsoft AI for Accessibility grant form
known_restrictions: Must use AI/technology for disability inclusion
recent_news: Active: Microsoft AI for Accessibility programme ongoing with rolling applications
funders.notes: TIMAMU TIER 1. Microsoft AI for Accessibility is the most accessible high-alignment grant for Timamu right now. Rolling applications. Timamu's assistive technology pathway is an exact match. Also explore Azure nonprofit credits. Apply as soon as Timamu has legal registration.

funders.name: CBM Global (Christian Blind Mission)
funders.mission_summary: International disability inclusion organisation and major funder/implementer of disability programmes globally. Active in East Africa. Funds disability-inclusive development, assistive technology, and inclusion programmes. Strong partner potential for Timamu — both as funder and implementation partner.
funders.notes: TIMAMU TIER 1. CBM has offices in East Africa. Both funder AND potential implementation partner — they have community networks Timamu can work through. CBM's partner-first model aligns exactly with Timamu's own model. Explore partnership before grant ask.

funders.name: Humanity & Inclusion (Handicap International)
funders.mission_summary: International NGO focused on disability inclusion and humanitarian action. Active in Tanzania. Implements disability inclusion programmes and also funds/partners with local organisations. Strong focus on economic inclusion for persons with disabilities.
funders.notes: TIMAMU TIER 1. HI Tanzania is active on the ground. Both potential funder and implementation partner. Digital inclusion for PWDs is their newer focus — Timamu fits exactly. Approach via HI Dar es Salaam office.

funders.name: Ford Foundation
funders.mission_summary: April 2025: Ford announced a $15M, 3-year global commitment to disability rights — their first major dedicated disability programme. Simultaneously committed that 25%+ of ALL Ford grantmaking will be disability-inclusive. Active Africa office. Partnering with Africa No Filter on creator economy content (Work Reimagined StoryLab) — direct overlap with Timamu's creator economy pathway.
recent_portfolio: Work Reimagined StoryLab with Africa No Filter
due_diligence_requirements: organisational led by persons with disabilities preferred
known_restrictions: Disability programme newer — more US-focused currently
funders.detected_priority_shift: 2025: Major new disability rights commitment — first in Ford's history
recent_news: April 2025: $15M disability rights commitment + 25% disability inclusion across all grantmaking
Partnership with Africa No Filter on Work Reimagined StoryLab
funders.notes: TIMAMU TIER 1. Ford's creator economy + Africa No Filter partnership is exactly Timamu's territory. Existing funder research profile in Drive (ID above). Technology + disability rights is Ford's new crossover. Entry: Ford Foundation Africa office, emphasise creator economy + disability rights = Timamu's model.

funders.name: Adobe Foundation
funders.mission_summary: Adobe Foundation funds creative and digital inclusion specifically for persons with disabilities. 'Creativity for all' is Adobe's mission. Grantees include organisations enabling PWDs to earn through digital creation. Timamu's Digital Creation pathway is an exact match.
recent_portfolio: Adobe UK Community Fund (£432K, 12 disability orgs)
recent_news: Adobe UK Community Fund: £432K to 12 disability orgs
TakingITGlobal $700K creativity grants in 18 countries
funders.notes: TIMAMU TIER 2 but HIGH ACCESSIBILITY. Small grants ($25-75K) but rolling applications, strong fit, and could be first anchor grant for Timamu. Also explore Adobe Creative Cloud donation programme for programme participants. Dual: grant + in-kind technology.

funders.name: Sida (Swedish International Development Cooperation Agency)
funders.mission_summary: Sweden's bilateral development agency. Tanzania is an active Sida country — recently signed SEK 78M research agreement with UDSM. Sweden ratified UN CRPD and disability mainstreaming is binding in all Sida programming. Digital transformation + disability inclusion = strong dual mandate fit.
recent_news: Active Tanzania programme. SEK 78M UDSM agreement.
funders.notes: TIMAMU. Swedish Embassy in Dar es Salaam is warm entry point. Also explore MyRight (Sida-funded Swedish DPO operating in East Africa) as co-applicant or sub-grant channel. Sida civil society track ($50-150K range) is most accessible for Phase 1 Timamu.

funders.name: MyRight (We believe in equal rights)
funders.mission_summary: Swedish disability rights organisation funded by Sida. Active in East Africa supporting local DPO organisations. Could be either a funder (sub-grant through Sida funds) or an implementation partner that brings Timamu into their network.
known_restrictions: DPO or disability rights focus required
funders.notes: TIMAMU. MyRight = gateway to Sida civil society funds AND DPO networks in Tanzania. Approach as partner first — they open doors to communities Timamu needs to serve. Could co-apply to Sida together.

funders.name: Open Society Foundations
funders.mission_summary: George Soros' global foundations network. Active in disability rights through the Open Society Initiative for Eastern Africa (OSIEA). Funds civil society, human rights, and disability inclusion. Tanzania is an active OSIEA country.
funders.notes: TIMAMU. OSIEA has active Tanzania programme. Human rights framing is the entry (UN CRPD + disability economic rights). Approach after Timamu legal registration is complete.

funders.name: Vodacom Foundation Tanzania
funders.mission_summary: Vodacom Tanzania Foundation with 80% technology mandate. Active in Tanzania. Digital inclusion for persons with disabilities is a natural fit. NOTE: VTF is already an active LearnImpact partner — cross-programme conflict flag applies. Cannot approach for Timamu while active LearnImpact relationship ongoing without deliberate coordination.
known_restrictions: CROSS-PROGRAMME CONFLICT: active LearnImpact partner. Do not approach for Timamu without explicit coordination decision.
funders.notes: ⚠️ CROSS-PROGRAMME CONFLICT. VTF = active LearnImpact partner (KiuFunza + SOMA pipeline). Cannot approach for Timamu simultaneously without deliberate coordination. Michael must decide which relationship takes priority. Sandra Oswald is the contact for BOTH entities — extreme care required.

funders.name: MTN Foundation Tanzania / MTN BRIGHT
funders.mission_summary: MTN Group's philanthropic arm. Education and digital skills focus across Africa. Tanzania MTN Foundation active. Digital inclusion and connectivity are strategic CSR priorities. Disability + digital = emerging priority.
funders.notes: TIMAMU. Smaller grants but accessible. Digital inclusion for PWDs in Tanzania is a good fit. Also explore MTN mobile money integration for programme participant earnings (not just funding — product partnership).

funders.name: Omidyar Network (Economic Inclusion)
funders.mission_summary: Omidyar Network's economic inclusion portfolio focuses on livelihoods, digital financial inclusion, and gig economy platforms. Timamu's gig economy + disability pathway (Phase 2) and creator economy pathway map to their portfolio. Also invests in Africa-focused technology ventures.
funders.notes: TIMAMU. Gig economy (Phase 2) and creator economy pathways map to Omidyar economic inclusion. Phase 1 Timamu may be too early — needs evidence of delivery. 2027-2028 target.

funders.name: FCDO (Disability Inclusion)
funders.mission_summary: FCDO has a disability inclusion commitment across all bilateral programming. Disability-inclusive digital development is an explicit priority. Tanzania FCDO has active programmes. Note: Same agency as LearnImpact's FCDO prospect — different programme streams, but same agency risk of over-exposure.
known_restrictions: ⚠️ Same agency as LearnImpact FCDO prospect — coordinate approaches
recent_news: ODA cuts impact Timamu track too — monitor
funders.notes: ⚠️ CROSS-PROGRAMME RISK. FCDO appears in both LearnImpact and Timamu lists — different programme streams (education vs disability inclusion) but same donor agency. Coordinate approaches carefully. Do not submit from both entities in same cycle without deliberate coordination.

funders.name: GIZ (Digital Transformation / Disability)
funders.mission_summary: GIZ's digital transformation programme includes digital inclusion and economic empowerment components. Disability mainstreaming is a requirement across GIZ programmes. Potential for technical assistance partnership rather than direct grant.
known_restrictions: ⚠️ Also appears in LearnImpact list — coordinate
funders.notes: TIMAMU + ⚠️ CROSS-PROGRAMME. Lower priority but worth monitoring. Technical assistance partnership with GIZ may be more realistic than direct grant for Phase 1 Timamu.

funders.name: International Disability Alliance (IDA)
funders.mission_summary: Global network of DPOs and disability rights organisations. Convenes disability rights actors globally and in East Africa. Less a traditional funder, more a legitimacy-building partner. Membership/affiliation could open doors to IDA-aligned bilateral donors.
funders.notes: TIMAMU. Value is primarily in legitimacy and network access rather than grant size. IDA affiliation signals Timamu's rights-based credentials to other disability funders. Low effort, worth pursuing.

funders.name: Tanzania Government — Creator Economy Fund
funders.mission_summary: Tanzania Vision 2050 has allocated TZS 2 billion specifically for online content creators (equipment, production, professional development) beginning July 2026. Digital and creative industries is a named priority sector. Timamu positions as capability partner — government funds infrastructure, Timamu builds economic capability for PWDs.

key_contacts.name: Ministry of Information, Communication and ICT Tanzania

funders.detected_priority_shift: July 2026: TZS 2B creator economy fund activates
recent_news: Tanzania Vision 2050: TZS 2B for creator economy begins July 2026
funders.notes: TIMAMU PRIORITY. This fund activates July 2026 — immediate action needed once Timamu is legally registered. Position: government funds the infrastructure and equipment; Timamu provides the disability-inclusive capability programme. PWD angle gives Timamu unique positioning versus general creator economy applicants.

funders.name: TaRL Africa
funders.mission_summary: Pan-African TaRL implementation arm. Operates in Tanzania directly. Collaborative with LearnImpact — not competitive. Potential partner for SOMA evidence legitimacy and network access. Also useful for navigating USAID and bilateral relationships.
recent_news: TaRL reached 80M children globally — now mainstream methodology
funders.notes: Not a funder — a partner/validator. TaRL Africa endorsement of SOMA's approach adds credibility with funders who back TaRL globally (Hewlett, Gates). Maintain collaborative relationship.

funders.name: Jacobs Foundation
funders.mission_summary: Swiss foundation focused on child and youth development. Funds research and evidence generation in education. Strong interest in Africa and teacher effectiveness. Funds J-PAL affiliated researchers and implementation partners.
funders.notes: Mtwara RCT is the entry point — Jacobs funds the type of rigorous evidence generation SOMA is doing. Partner with UDSM or J-PAL to strengthen research credentials. Apply to October cycle once Mtwara baseline is designed and pre-registered.

funders.name: International Education and Quality (IEQ) Funders
funders.mission_summary: Category: Smaller evidence-based education funders including CIFF (Children's Investment Fund Foundation), co-impact, and the LEGO Foundation. Each has specific alignment points with SOMA or KiuFunza.
funders.notes: Placeholder for CIFF, Co-Impact, LEGO Foundation — each warrants individual research. Add as separate funder records in weekly research phase.

funders.name: GSM Foundation (GSM Group)
funders.mission_summary: Corporate social responsibility foundation of GSM Group (Tanzania), led by President Ghalib Said Mohamed. Operates across all regions of mainland Tanzania and Zanzibar. Has reached 500,000+ students through education support, classroom construction, and inclusive education initiatives. Also funds healthcare, environment, and community empowerment.
known_restrictions: likely prefers tangible/visible impact over systems change
typically infrastructure and access rather than pedagogical programmes
funders.notes: Local Tanzanian corporate foundation. SOMA pitch angle: Mwalimu Kinara (teacher recognition — visible, community-facing) + learning dashboard (tangible data product). Frame around number of students reached, not evidence methodology. Direct contact: foundation@gsmgroup.africa | +255 745 750 602. Short application process likely. Michael may have a direct relationship entry point given LI Tanzania presence.

funders.name: Standard Chartered Futuremakers (Tanzania)
funders.mission_summary: Standard Chartered's flagship community investment programme, active in 35 markets including Tanzania. Focuses on economic inclusion of disadvantaged youth (18–35): digital skills, financial literacy, and employability. Does not fund directly — partners with local NGOs as implementing organisations. Annual commitment per implementing partner: typically USD 50K–200K over 1–2 years.
recent_portfolio: Futuremakers implementing partners across East Africa
known_restrictions: partner model — SC does not fund passively; requires co-designed programme
focus on 18–35 age group; primary school age is a stretch
funders.notes: Tanzania SC office in Dar es Salaam. Futuremakers operates through NGO implementing partners, not open grants. Approach: request an introductory meeting with SC Tanzania Corporate Affairs team. SOMA angle: frame Mwalimu Kinara as teacher economic empowerment + the learning dashboard as a digital public good. The 'youth' frame is a stretch for primary teachers but can be made to work. Worth one meeting.

funders.name: KCB Foundation (Tanzania)
funders.mission_summary: The philanthropic arm of KCB Group, one of East Africa's largest banking groups with a strong Tanzania presence. KCB Foundation focuses on education, environment, and entrepreneurship (2jiajiri programme). Has funded secondary and vocational education NGOs across Tanzania. Operates through an annual grants cycle and direct partnerships.
known_restrictions: focus tends toward access/bursaries rather than system-strengthening
funders.notes: KCB Bank Tanzania branch in Dar es Salaam is the entry point. Request meeting with Country Manager + Corporate Affairs. SOMA pitch: Mwalimu Kinara (teacher recognition = visible, community-reportable) + student learning data dashboard. Lead with number of schools and student reach, not methodology. Accessible funder with lower bar than international foundations.

funders.name: Equity Group Foundation
funders.mission_summary: The philanthropic arm of Equity Group Holdings (East Africa's largest bank by customer count). Operates Wings to Fly scholarship programme and broader education, health, and enterprise development. Tanzania operations exist through Equity Bank Tanzania though the foundation is more Kenya-focused. Has supported 60,000+ students through education programmes.
known_restrictions: foundation primarily Kenya-focused; Tanzania operations are secondary
tends toward scholarship/access rather than system-strengthening
funders.notes: Entry point: Equity Bank Tanzania Managing Director. Foundation is Nairobi-based but awards Tanzania grants. SOMA angle: position learning assessment system as complementary to scholarship selection (Equity uses learning metrics for scholarship allocation). Lower priority than KCB or SC given Kenya-primary focus.

funders.name: CRDB Bank Foundation
funders.mission_summary: Subsidiary of CRDB Bank (Tanzania's largest bank by assets). Focuses on entrepreneurship for youth and women (iMBEJU programme — 400,000+ financially included), financial literacy, health (maternity wards), and climate action. Primary focus is economic empowerment rather than educational quality, but has funded community learning initiatives. Seed capital to entrepreneurs is the core programme.
known_restrictions: not a natural fit for SOMA unless Mwalimu Kinara framing used
funders.notes: Contact: info@crdbbankfoundation.co.tz / +255 222 117442. Lower priority. SOMA angle if approached: financial literacy for teachers (Mwalimu Kinara economic benefit) + data dashboard as community transparency tool. Weaker fit than GSM Foundation or KCB. Worth one email.

funders.name: Tanzania Breweries Limited (AB InBev) — Social Investment
funders.mission_summary: Tanzania Breweries Limited (TBL) is a subsidiary of AB InBev, the world's largest brewer. TBL's social investment programme covers education, water access, agriculture, and community empowerment across Tanzania. AB InBev's global 100+ Accelerator and Smart Agriculture programmes have Tanzania components. Education and youth are consistent CSR pillars.
funders.notes: EXCLUDED — alcohol industry. LearnImpact board policy: no funding from alcohol or related industries. Do not approach.

funders.name: AngloGold Ashanti — Geita Gold Mine Community Fund
funders.mission_summary: AngloGold Ashanti operates Geita Gold Mine, one of Africa's largest gold mines, in Geita Region, Tanzania. Has a Community Social Investment (CSI) programme focused on communities near the mine: education, health, livelihoods, and infrastructure. Funds are geographically restricted to Geita Region only. Education includes school construction, bursaries, and teacher support.
known_restrictions: currently no active LI schools in Geita — re-entry requires funding to cover it
mining company CSI is geographically bound to Geita Region
funders.notes: LearnImpact has historical KiuFunza presence in Geita Region from previous phases — not currently active but can re-establish if funding allows. This makes AngloGold Geita a real opportunity: credible NGO with proven track record in the region. If a conversation opens with Geita Gold Mine Community Affairs, frame re-entry into Geita as part of the funding scope. Contact: Geita Gold Mine Community Affairs office, Geita Town.

funders.name: Barrick Gold — North Mara & Bulyanhulu Community Funds
funders.mission_summary: Barrick Gold operates North Mara Gold Mine (Mara Region) and Bulyanhulu Gold Mine (Kahama District, Shinyanga Region) in Tanzania. Both operations have Community Development Funds focused on communities within the mine's area of influence. Education, water, health, and livelihoods are funded. Geographically restricted to Mara and Shinyanga regions.
due_diligence_requirements: geographic presence in Mara or Shinyanga
known_restrictions: geographically restricted to Mara Region (North Mara) and Shinyanga Region (Bulyanhulu)
funders.notes: Only relevant where KiuFunza/SOMA has or can establish schools in Mara or Shinyanga regions. Two entry points: North Mara Community Affairs (Tarime/Rorya, Mara Region) and Bulyanhulu Community Affairs (Kahama District, Shinyanga Region). SOMA pitch: Mwalimu Kinara teacher recognition + learning dashboard as visible, community-reportable investment. Mining community funds often undersubscribed for credible NGO partners — worth approaching directly.

funders.name: Halotel (Viettel Tanzania) — Social Investment
funders.mission_summary: Halotel is the Tanzania brand of Viettel, the Vietnamese state-owned telecoms company. Fourth-largest mobile operator in Tanzania with growing rural coverage. Has a social investment programme focused on education, digital connectivity, and rural community development — particularly in underserved areas where they're building towers. Has supplied ICT equipment to schools and funded education events.
geography: focus on rural and underserved areas
known_restrictions: state-owned by Vietnam — funding decisions can be slow
stronger on digital/ICT than pedagogical approach
funders.notes: Halotel has strong rural Tanzania reach — maps well to KiuFunza school geography. SOMA pitch: learning progress dashboard (digital product) + AI analytics = ICT in education angle. Frame as technology-enabled teacher support, not as a teaching methodology. Accessible entry point. Contact Halotel Tanzania HQ in Dar es Salaam.
